mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold, invalidating the negative earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer discretionary and residential investment demand, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day S&P returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 6-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side slack, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during INDPRO contractions, or the signal predictive power vanishes when tested against a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate compression and accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures, which boosts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL spread shows no significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate wage income and consumer demand, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P returns in over 60% of cases, or the series fails to produce a negative Rank IC greater than 0.025.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% signals robust economic momentum that exceeds baseline expectations, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns show no positive skew following GDPC1 releases above 2.5%, or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates capacity utilization exhaustion and impending earnings deterioration, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of late-cycle INDPRO decline episodes, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.
